body.title: weekly-retro 2026-07-10
body.text:
## Week ending 2026-07-10

### Hypothesis confirmation rate
0 outcomes recorded this week (0 confirmed / 0 falsified). 269 designs+decisions
carry no /outcome at all. Normally this would flag "the outcome loop has stopped
closing" — and it HAD stopped, but with a now-diagnosed root cause: the
/outcome -> /weekly-retro loop bug (outcome_date written to decisions/ while retro
reads designs/-first + dedup -> the canonical design was perpetually unreviewed).
That bug was FIXED today (@193653aa): /outcome now writes to the canonical design
(re-projecting the decision), verified end-to-end. So the zero is explained, not
mysterious — the machinery that would record outcomes was mis-wired until today.

### Priority changes for next week
The outcome loop is now mechanically capable of closing for the first time. Next
week: start actually running /outcome on recently-shipped designs (begin with this
week's INTENTION->DESIGN->TICKET stack once they've had a few days to show whether
the intention held) so the confirmation-rate signal becomes real rather than
structurally-zero. No bet looks wrong yet — we simply have no outcome data because
the recorder was broken; fixing that is the unblock.

### Needs /outcome
269 records carry no outcome — but the meaningful, actionable subset is this week's
shipped designs (design-first artifact type, intention capture/deconstruct, reader
migration). The long legacy tail predates the outcome-loop machinery and is not
individually overdue in a useful sense. Do NOT bulk-/outcome the tail; run it going
forward on freshly-shipped designs.
